steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the quotation that most directly supports the claim in the text. The passage's claim is that the "overly object-focused" view is wrong because Stevens strove to convey the ephemeral and dynamic characteristics of ikaah as a cultural practice—not through the static objects themselves, but through his actions.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "Stevens's ambassadorial sandpaintings are best understood not as self-contained objects but as reminders of the public creations of the sandpaintings, during which Stevens conducted appropriate *ikaah* rituals and encouraged viewers to closely track his movements and subtle shifts in the sand throughout the process.": Shifts focus to the creation process and viewer observation—this supports ephemerality indirectly but does not address Stevens's role as gift-giver or the transmission of cultural knowledge. ✗
  • Choice B — "While Stevens's ambassadorial sandpaintings are undoubtedly educative for audiences that have little exposure to Diné culture, they should not be confused with authentic *ikaah*, which cannot be extricated from a practice that is intentionally and necessarily transitory nor condensed into a single persistent object.": Contradicts the passage; the text explicitly argues against the view that static objects cannot authentically represent ikaah. ✗
  • Choice C — "The most compelling way to reconcile the apparent tension between the temporary and performative nature of *ikaah* and the persistent and static nature of Stevens's ambassadorial sandpaintings is to recognize that Stevens was an ambassador not only of Diné culture but of a US art culture that tended to value permanent works over ephemeral ones.": Offers an alternative explanation that contradicts the passage's framing; the text never attributes any US art-culture bias to Stevens. ✗
  • Choice D — "It is important to recall Stevens's own actions as a gift-giver—a temporary and performative role—of the sandpaintings, for by taking those actions, Stevens publicly enacted the transmission of knowledge of both traditional *ikaah* practices and cutting-edge chemistry (in the form of the preservative additives), a hybrid that reflects the dynamism of Diné culture.": Directly supports the passage by identifying Stevens's actions (not the objects) as temporary and performative, and by showing how the transmission of both traditional ikaah knowledge and innovative chemistry reflects Diné dynamism. ✓
The quoted gift-giver sentence is the correct support.
